text: When setting project goals, it is important to use the SMART framework. This means your goals should be Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. For example, instead of saying "improve the website," a SMART goal would be "increase website traffic by 20% within three months." To stay on track, break your project into milestones and define clear deliverables at each stage. Milestones are key checkpoints, while deliverables are the actual results or products you produce, such as a report or a design. Finally, ensure stakeholder alignment by discussing goals with everyone involved. This way, all team members and decision-makers agree on the objectives and timeline from the start.
